sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Believes He Got the Last Laugh With Home Depot (HD) Stock
feed.summary: Jim Cramer expressed satisfaction with his decision to buy Home Depot (HD) stock for his charitable trust, stating that critics are "not laughing now." Analysts from Truist and TD Cowen have raised their price targets for Home Depot (HD) in January, maintaining Buy ratings due to strong card data, holiday updates, potential tax refund benefits, and its strong market positioning. Despite recent stock fluctuations, Cramer's conviction reinforces bullish sentiment among some financial professionals.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Admits He Got Emerson (EMR) Wrong
feed.summary: Jim Cramer of CNBC admitted he was wrong about Emerson Electric Co. (EMR), acknowledging that its acquisitions, which he initially opposed, actually worked. He now sees Emerson as a strong performer, likening it to Eaton, benefiting from its automation and software products, and exposure to growth markets like data centers. Analysts at JPMorgan and Evercore ISI have also expressed positive views on EMR, with Evercore setting a $170 share price target due to the company's strategic initiatives and portfolio quality improvements.

feed.title: PayPal (PYPL) is Facing Stiff Competition From Apple, Believes Jim Cramer
feed.summary: Jim Cramer believes PayPal (PYPL) is facing severe competition from Apple Pay, which he considers a superior product that has "obliterated" PayPal. This comes as PayPal's shares have significantly declined over the past year and year-to-date, with analysts like Truist and RBC Capital lowering price targets due to sluggish volume growth and take rate compression. Cramer suggests PayPal needs to change its shareholder base and develop new offerings to stop the bleeding against Apple's strong payment solution.

feed.title: Jim Cramer Discusses Eli Lilly (LLY) & Weight Loss Drugs
feed.summary: Jim Cramer highlighted Eli Lilly (LLY) and its strong performance in the weight loss drug market, noting its lead over Novo Nordisk. The company reported impressive Q4 and FY2025 earnings, leading to upgraded price targets from Freedom Capital and Deutsche Bank. Cramer specifically mentioned the success of Eli Lilly's weight loss pill, which is gaining popularity due to its ease of use compared to injections.

feed.title: TC Energy Forecasts Natural Gas Demand Surge Equivalent to Entire European Market
feed.summary: TC Energy projects a significant increase in North American natural gas demand, estimating a jump of 45 Bcf/d by 2035. This surge is primarily attributed to rising LNG exports, increased power generation needs, and the growing demand for reliability from distribution companies. The forecast highlights natural gas's critical role in meeting future energy requirements across North America.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Co (NYSE:VMC) Lags Estimates
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Co (NYSE:VMC) reported fourth-quarter earnings below analyst expectations, with a 9% decline in gross profit. Despite the quarterly setback, the company achieved full-year net earnings growth of 18% and a 7% increase in annual revenue. Looking to 2026, Vulcan anticipates continued strength in public construction and improving private nonresidential opportunities, projecting adjusted EBITDA between $2.4 billion and $2.6 billion.

feed.title: Republic Services Inc (NYSE:RSG) Posts Mixed Result
feed.summary: Republic Services Inc (NYSE:RSG) stock declined 4.67% after reporting mixed results for the fourth quarter of FY25. While net income and adjusted EPS surpassed analyst estimates, revenue growth of 2.2% to $4.14 billion missed expectations due to organic decline in environmental solutions and a decrease in recycled commodity prices. Despite mixed performance, the company projects revenue between $17.050 billion and $17.150 billion for fiscal year 2026, with adjusted diluted EPS expected to be $7.20 to $7.28.

feed.title: Medtronic PLC (NYSE:MDT) Margin Contracts
feed.summary: Medtronic PLC (NYSE:MDT) reported strong third-quarter fiscal 2026 results, exceeding analyst expectations with significant revenue growth across its portfolios, especially in Cardiovascular. Despite overall strong performance and beating EPS and revenue estimates, the company's gross margin and adjusted operating margin contracted due to increased cost of products sold. Medtronic maintained its fiscal year 2026 guidance, expecting continued organic revenue growth and stable adjusted EPS.

feed.title: Newton Golf adds finance veteran to board
feed.summary: Newton Golf Company has appointed John Bode to its board of directors, increasing the number of independent directors to three. Bode brings extensive financial oversight from public companies and direct golf industry experience, including owning three golf clubs and founding Maxim Golf Solutions. This appointment follows a record revenue performance in 2025 for Newton Golf, which is focused on product line and international expansion.

feed.title: EQT Corp (NYSE:EQT) Posts Strong Production
feed.summary: EQT Corp reported strong fourth-quarter earnings and revenue, surpassing analyst expectations due to increased production, improved pricing, and reduced costs. The company's sales volumes and free cash flow were robust, and it beat EPS and revenue estimates. EQT also provided a positive outlook for 2026, forecasting strong production and significant free cash flow.

feed.title: Devon Energy Corp ( NYSE:DVN) Gave Downbeat Guidance
feed.summary: Devon Energy Corp (NYSE:DVN) stock rose slightly after beating fourth-quarter profit estimates, but forecast lower first-quarter production due to severe winter storms. The company recently announced a $58 billion merger with Coterra Energy, aiming for $1 billion in annual pre-tax savings by 2027. Despite the production forecast, Devon reported increased fourth-quarter production and improvements in its financial position, including reduced long-term debt and beating revenue expectations.

feed.title: Cadence Design Systems Inc (NASDAQ:CDNS) Posts Record Backlog
feed.summary: Cadence Design Systems Inc. (NASDAQ: CDNS) reported better-than-expected Q4 FY25 results, with its stock rising 9.44%. The company finished 2025 with a record backlog of $7.8 billion and announced collaborations with TSMC and Intel Foundry. Cadence's core EDA business, hardware business, IP business, and System Design and Analysis business all showed strong growth, and the company provided an optimistic outlook for fiscal 2026.

feed.title: Coca-Cola investment in province creating 500 jobs
feed.summary: Coca-Cola Canada Bottling Ltd. is investing $141 million to expand its Brampton, Ont. facility, which is projected to create up to 500 construction jobs and enhance Ontario's manufacturing and agri-food sectors. The project will add a new advanced production line, bolstering the province's supply chain capacity and reinforcing its economic strength. Ontario officials, including Premier Doug Ford, lauded the investment as a significant endorsement of the province's workforce and economic policies.

feed.title: Caesars launches Ca$hline slot game across online casino platforms
feed.summary: Caesars Entertainment (NASDAQ:CZR) has introduced a new three-reel slot game called Ca$hline on its online casino platforms in New Jersey, developed by its in-house studio, Empire Creative. The game offers flexible gameplay with features like a 2x multiplier and a wheel bonus. Despite a significant stock price decline, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is currently undervalued, following a mixed Q4 earnings report with a wider-than-expected loss but increased revenue, driven by strong digital performance.

feed.title: Northern Trust names Jessica Donohue as head of Asset Servicing, Americas
feed.summary: Northern Trust has appointed Jessica Donohue as head of Asset Servicing, Americas. Donohue previously served as global head of product management for Asset Servicing and held senior leadership positions at State Street. The financial services firm has seen its shares climb 32.92% over the past year and reported strong Q4 2025 earnings.

feed.title: Assessing Southwest Airlines (LUV) Valuation After A Strong Multi‑Period Share Price Rally
feed.summary: This article analyzes Southwest Airlines (LUV) valuation after significant share price gains, noting a discrepancy between a popular narrative estimating the company as 14.3% overvalued at $54.26, and a DCF model suggesting a 59.9% discount. The narrative anticipates strong earnings growth from loyalty program optimization and partnership agreements, while the DCF model projects a fair value of $135.47 per share. The article encourages investors to examine the underlying assumptions to form their own conclusions.

feed.title: New Charles River Leaders May Shape Valuation, Debt And Governance Outlook
feed.summary: Charles River Laboratories International (NYSE:CRL) has appointed Glenn G. Coleman as Chief Financial Officer and Kerry Dailey as Chief Legal Officer. These new leadership roles are expected to influence the company's financial oversight, capital allocation, debt management, and regulatory compliance, particularly given the company's current high debt level. Investors will be watching how these executives shape decision-making and report on these matters in future communications.

feed.title: EQT buys larger stake in Mountain Valley Pipeline
feed.summary: EQT, in a joint venture with Blackstone Credit and Insurance, is set to acquire Con Edison's stake in a pipeline for $213 million. This transaction involves exercising an option to buy compression and mainline assets, and is expected to close this year. The article highlights EQT's increased investment in the Mountain Valley Pipeline.

feed.title: GE HealthCare and BARDA outlay $35m to expand AI ultrasound development contract
feed.summary: GE HealthCare and BARDA have expanded their contract by $35 million to further develop AI-powered ultrasound systems. This expansion focuses on enhancing imaging for trauma assessment and mass casualty incidents, aiming to improve diagnostic speed and reduce reliance on expert users, especially in challenging field environments. The initiative builds on a previous $44 million agreement and seeks to generate clinical evidence for next-generation point-of-care ultrasound technology with new AI applications.

feed.title: S&P Global Stock (SPGI) Opinions on Q4 Earnings Selloff
feed.summary: S&P Global (SPGI) shares plunged over 13% pre-market following Q4 2025 earnings due to weaker-than-expected 2026 guidance, despite solid revenue growth. Social media discussions highlighted investor sensitivity to guidance miss but also noted the stock's rare discount for dip-buying, supported by recurring revenue and potential AI tailwinds. Insider purchases and congressional trading also revealed activity around the stock.

feed.title: 10 Consumer Discretionary Stocks With Whale Alerts In Today's Session
feed.summary: This article identifies 10 Consumer Discretionary stocks that have triggered "whale alerts" due to unusual options activity. It details specific call and put option sweeps for companies like Amazon, Tesla, Nike, and Alibaba, indicating significant trading interest from large investors. The report provides key options data, including strike prices, expiration dates, and contract volumes, to help traders understand these potential market opportunities.

feed.title: Coca-Cola (KO) Experiences Mixed Options Activity Amid Price Dip
feed.summary: Coca-Cola (KO) recently experienced a slight stock price dip, accompanied by mixed options activity where call options were favored over puts. Despite a decrease in implied volatility, it remains above the 52-week median, indicating ongoing market interest. The company maintains strong financial health, moderate volatility, and high institutional ownership, although insider selling transactions suggest some caution.

feed.title: New $6.6M power hub to reduce outages for 2,400 WV customers
feed.summary: FirstEnergy's subsidiary, Potomac Edison, has completed a new $6.6 million substation in Berkeley County, West Virginia. The facility, finished in December, serves approximately 2,400 customers in Falling Waters and Spring Mills, aiming to reduce outages, speed up restoration, and alleviate capacity issues from older substations. This project is part of FirstEnergy's larger Energize365 investment program, which plans to invest $36 billion between 2026 and 2030 to modernize and strengthen the electric grid.

feed.title: Microsoft, CrowdStrike expand deal to allow all customers Falcon on Microsoft Marketplace access (MSFT:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: Microsoft and CrowdStrike have expanded their existing partnership, enabling companies of all sizes to purchase CrowdStrike's Falcon cybersecurity platform directly through the Microsoft Marketplace. This collaboration allows customers to leverage their existing Microsoft Azure Consumption Commitment (MACC) for these purchases. The integration aims to simplify procurement and enhance cybersecurity solutions for businesses.

feed.title: TWELVE DIRECTORS SLATED FOR GRAINGER'S BOARD TO BE VOTED ON AT THE COMPANY'S ANNUAL MEETING ON APRIL 29, 2026
feed.summary: Grainger (NYSE: GWW) has announced the slate of twelve nominees for its Board of Directors for the 2026-2027 term. All current Board members, these candidates will be voted on at the Annual Meeting of Shareholders on April 29, 2026. D.G. Macpherson, Grainger Chairman and CEO, highlighted the Board's diverse perspectives and commitment to creating long-term value for stakeholders.

feed.title: Insulet (PODD) Shares Skyrocket, What You Need To Know
feed.summary: Insulet (PODD) shares jumped 5.6% after reporting strong fourth-quarter 2025 financial results that exceeded Wall Street's expectations, driven by high demand for its Omnipod insulin management systems. The company also provided a positive outlook, projecting a 26% year-over-year sales increase for the first quarter of 2026. Despite this positive news, Insulet's stock is still down 7.9% since the beginning of the year and trading below its 52-week high.

feed.title: Why Camping World (CWH) Stock Is Trading Up Today
feed.summary: Camping World (CWH) stock rose by 4.6% after LCI Industries, a major RV component supplier, reported strong Q4 results, indicating robust demand in the RV market. LCI Industries' 16% net sales growth and 96% net income surge suggested more RVs are being built, boosting investor confidence in the sector and lifting Camping World's shares. Despite this positive movement, the stock remains significantly below its 52-week high, and past concerns about its balance sheet and cash flow still linger.

feed.title: SM Energy to sell some Texas assets for $950 million
feed.summary: SM Energy Company announced the sale of some of its South Texas assets to Caturus for $950 million. The company, an independent energy producer, focuses on oil, gas, and natural gas liquid production primarily in the Midland Basin of West Texas and the Maverick Basin of South Texas. This sale reflects SM Energy's ongoing portfolio management and strategic adjustments within the energy sector.

feed.title: Why Is CarMax (KMX) Stock Soaring Today
feed.summary: Shares of CarMax (KMX) jumped 7.4% after an analyst at Baird raised the company's price target to $48 from $42, maintaining an Outperform rating. This more optimistic valuation was influenced by industry forecasts predicting a favorable market for used car buyers in 2026 due to improved inventory levels. Despite the recent rise, CarMax's stock is still trading 49% below its 52-week high from February 2025.

feed.title: Philips Settles Suit Over Sleep Machine Starting Home Fire
feed.summary: Medical equipment company Philips has reached a settlement in a North Carolina federal court with a woman who sued them over a sleep apnea breathing machine. The woman alleged that the device caught fire, causing burns to her mother's face and destroying her home. The article mentions the date of record for the specific article is February 17, 2026, 1:41 PM EST.

feed.title: UBS raises Gilead Sciences stock price target on HIV pipeline data
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) to $175 from $155, maintaining a Buy rating, based on upcoming Phase I data for long-acting HIV treatment candidates. The firm highlighted Gilead's strategy to introduce six-month treatments to transition patients from daily oral medications, emphasizing the company's leadership in the $15 billion HIV treatment market. This follows Gilead's strong fourth-quarter revenue and adjusted EPS, which surpassed expectations due to robust sales in HIV and oncology segments.

feed.title: Apa stock hits 52-week high at $28.56
feed.summary: APA Corporation's stock has reached a new 52-week high of $28.56, reflecting a strong performance with significant price returns over the last year. The company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score, appears undervalued by InvestingPro, and has a consistent dividend payment history, with its next earnings report due in seven days. Analysts from Benchmark, RBC Capital, Wolfe Research, and William Blair have provided varying price targets and ratings, highlighting the company's strategic initiatives and asset base.

feed.title: Apple to kill app support for Intel-based Macs next year
feed.summary: Apple has announced that macOS 26 will be the last operating system version to support Intel-based Macs, and Rosetta support for Intel-based apps will end with macOS 28 in 2027. This means companies and users relying on Intel apps or Macs need to prepare for upgrades or replacements to ensure business continuity and security. While security updates will continue for some older Mac models for two years post-support, and Rosetta will remain for certain unmaintained games, most developers have already transitioned to Apple Silicon, and Apple sees this move as a benefit for performance and productivity.

feed.title: PPL Analyst Rating Update: BMO Capital Raises Price Target | PPL Stock News
feed.summary: BMO Capital has raised its price target for PPL (PPL) from $40.00 to $41.00, maintaining an "Outperform" rating. This update comes amidst several recent analyst adjustments, with the average target price from 14 analysts now standing at $40.11, implying a 7.74% upside from its current price. GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $35.96 for PPL, suggesting a 3.4% downside.

feed.title: 5 Financial Transaction Stocks to Watch Despite Elevated Expense Level
feed.summary: The financial transaction services industry is poised for growth due to expanding global trade, e-commerce, and demand for cross-border payments, despite rising technology costs and inflation impacting consumer spending. Zacks Investment Research identifies five companies—Global Payments (GPN), Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), Fiserv (FISV), and Fidelity National Information Services (FIS)—as strong contenders for long-term growth, citing their strategic initiatives, M&A activities, and robust earnings outlooks. The industry faces challenges from elevated expenses in technology and cybersecurity but is buoyed by a resilient labor market and potential interest rate cuts in 2026.

feed.title: Federal Realty’s Retail Formula: Steady Growth, Durable Dividends
feed.summary: Federal Realty Investment Trust (FRT) focuses on open-air shopping centers and mixed-use developments in affluent markets, demonstrating steady growth and a commitment to its dividend. The company achieved record leasing volume in 2025 and maintains high occupancy, with a strong balance sheet supported by investment-grade ratings despite elevated leverage. FRT is notable for its "Dividend King" status, having consistently increased its dividend for decades, making it an attractive option for income investors seeking resilience and durability.

feed.title: Prudential Issues New Shares Under 2023 Employee Sharesave Plan
feed.summary: Prudential plc has issued 1,357 new ordinary shares under its 2023 Prudential Sharesave Plan, utilizing an existing block admission to the London Stock Exchange. This move aims to support employee ownership and incentives across its Asian and African growth markets with minimal dilution to existing shareholders. Analysts currently rate Prudential's stock as a Buy with a price target of £13.20.

feed.title: Former NetApp CTO wins dismissal of lawsuit after Vast Data deal
feed.summary: A Florida judge has dismissed NetApp's lawsuit against its former CTO, Jonsi Stefánsson, ruling that the case should be litigated in Iceland due to a mandatory forum selection clause in his employment contract. NetApp had accused Stefánsson of breach of contract and stealing trade secrets after he left the company to form Red Stapler, which was subsequently acquired by Vast Data. The court found that Stefánsson's employment agreement and a proprietary information agreement needed to be read together, with the Icelandic jurisdiction clause taking precedence.

feed.title: Goldman CEO Solomon, Long a Crypto Skeptic, Says He Owns Bitcoin
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs CEO David Solomon, previously a cryptocurrency skeptic, revealed he now owns a "very, very limited" amount of Bitcoin. He made this disclosure at the World Liberty Forum at Mar-a-Lago in Florida on Wednesday. This marks a shift in his public stance on cryptocurrencies.

feed.title: Hormel to Sell Whole-Bird Turkey Business
feed.summary: Hormel Foods Corp. has announced it will sell its whole-bird turkey business to Life-Science Innovations to reduce exposure to commodity market volatility. The sale includes a plant, feed mill, and transportation assets, but Hormel retains its Jennie-O brand. This move aligns with Hormel's strategy to focus on value-added proteins, especially in light of recent avian influenza outbreaks.

feed.title: Jefferies Adjusts Price Target on Textron to $115 From $110, Maintains Buy Rating
feed.summary: Jefferies has increased its price target for Textron (TXT) to $115 from the previous $110, while reiterating a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment reflects a positive outlook from the analyst firm on the diversified industrial group, which specializes in aircraft, helicopters, industrial equipment, and defense systems. This news follows several other recent analyst adjustments and company announcements, including an equity buyback.

feed.title: MasTec Schedules Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2025 Earnings Conference Call
feed.summary: MasTec, Inc. (NYSE: MTZ) announced it will release its fourth quarter and full year financial results for 2025 on Thursday, February 26, 2026, after market close. Senior management will host a webcast the following day, Friday, February 27, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. ET, to discuss these results. The webcast will be available on MasTec's Investor Relations website.

feed.title: Booking Holdings Links Route 66 Centennial Deal To Valuation Story
feed.summary: Booking Holdings, through Booking.com, has become the Official Travel Partner for the Route 66 Centennial, set for 2026. This partnership is seen as a strategic move to boost brand visibility, particularly in domestic and road-trip segments, and could inform future product development in travel planning tools and cross-selling. While primarily a branding and customer acquisition play, investors should monitor its impact on US booking volumes and user engagement, especially given Booking Holdings' identified risk of high debt.

feed.title: Qualcomm's Analysts Are Throwing in the Towel-Time to Be Brave?
feed.summary: Shares of Qualcomm have significantly declined, prompting analysts to grow cautious, with some downgrading the stock amid concerns about its growth trajectory and exposure to the smartphone cycle. However, technical indicators suggest the stock is oversold, and some analysts maintain a bullish outlook, presenting a potential contrarian buying opportunity for investors willing to take a risk. The $135 level is identified as key support, with its ability to hold determining the stock's near-term direction.

feed.title: CoStar: U.S. retail vacancy expected to rise ‘minimally’ in first half of 2026
feed.summary: CoStar forecasts that U.S. retail vacancy will rise minimally in the first half of 2026 before declining slightly later in the year and into 2027. Despite receding, store closures are anticipated to increase in early 2026 as a bifurcated retail sales environment prompts some tenants to reduce locations. The stable outlook is supported by a resumption of positive demand in late 2025, although downside risks related to tariffs and potential price increases remain.

feed.title: WEC Energy Group SVP Straka sells $325k in stock
feed.summary: Mary Beth Straka, Senior VP at WEC Energy Group (NASDAQ:WEC), sold 2,815 shares of company stock for over $325,000 after exercising stock options. This transaction follows WEC Energy Group's recent financial results, which included an EPS miss but strong revenue exceeding forecasts and upgraded price targets from analysts. The company is noted for 56 consecutive years of dividend payments and a current dividend yield of 3.3%.

feed.title: Wec Energy director Klappa sells $3.47 million in stock
feed.summary: Gale E. Klappa, a director at WEC Energy Group, sold 30,000 shares of common stock for approximately $3.47 million in February 2026, while also exercising options to acquire 30,000 shares for $2,045,250. Following these transactions, Klappa directly owns 276,600 shares of the company. These insider transactions occurred amidst WEC Energy reporting Q4 2025 financial results that missed EPS expectations but exceeded revenue forecasts, with analysts maintaining positive ratings and the company projecting future EPS growth.

feed.title: Form 424B2 GOLDMAN SACHS GROUP INC
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has filed Form 424B2 for $205,000,000 Floating Rate Notes due 2029. These notes will pay interest at a rate of compounded SOFR plus 0.86% per annum, with a minimum interest rate of 0.00%, payable quarterly. The filing details the specific terms, risk factors related to the structure, valuation, and market sales, as well as U.S. federal income tax consequences for investors.

feed.title: DEVON ENERGY CORP/DE SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Devon Energy Corporation has released its 2025 Form 10-K report, detailing financial performance, business operations, and strategic initiatives. The report highlights stable revenues, decreased net earnings due to lower prices, and significant production increases, particularly in the Delaware Basin. Key strategies include a business optimization plan, the Williston Basin acquisition, a planned merger with Coterra for future growth, and active capital management through share repurchases and debt reduction.

feed.title: Apa stock hits 52-week high at $28.56 By Investing.com
feed.summary: APA Corporation's stock reached a new 52-week high at $28.56, reflecting a strong performance with a 36.74% return over the past six months and a 20.52% increase over the last year. The company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score and is considered undervalued, with analysts reiterating positive ratings and price targets. Upcoming earnings and strategic developments, including the sale of the Allgas network, are expected to influence future performance.
